purpose: Remediate the L-2 preregistration before execution by giving the candidate and matched comparator one explicit decision index, signal return window, execution index, and active-return window without changing locked v2.
owner_authorization: The owner explicitly authorized B5.2 on 2026-07-24 to remediate the L-2 time-index contract, with no backtest, validation access, broker/provider call, or paid action.

superseded_artifact.remediation: v2 did not make the candidate and matched comparator's shared decision-index return window explicit, leaving an off-by-one implementation ambiguity.

inherits_v2.inherits: Every v2 field except the candidate/comparator time-index wording and this explicit time_index_contract, including universe, timing calendar, sizing, costs, return accounting, primary utility, DSR, MinTRL, decision matrix, and validation seal.

candidate_signal.name: equal_weight_multi_lookback_t_stat
candidate_signal.shared_decision_return_window: At decision close t, for every horizon h use exactly r[t-k] for k=0..h-1; r[t] is the latest admissible return and r[t+1] or later is forbidden.
candidate_signal.component_formula: t_stat_h[t] = mean(r[t-k] for k=0..h-1) divided by the sample standard deviation of that same set times sqrt(h); require h complete observations.
candidate_signal.combined_signal_formula: q_tstat[t] = arithmetic mean of tanh(t_stat_h[t]) across the locked horizons.

primary.shared_decision_return_window: At the same decision close t and for each same horizon h, use exactly r[t-k] for k=0..h-1; the set must equal the candidate component's set and cannot contain r[t+1] or later.
primary.component_formula: q_count_h[t] = sum(sign(r[t-k]) for k=0..h-1) / h, using the inherited directional-count zero and missing-return rules.
primary.combined_signal_formula: q_count_matched[t] = arithmetic mean of q_count_h[t] across the locked horizons.
primary.role: Primary comparator only; it differs from the candidate only by the signal transform.

secondary.role: Secondary descriptive reference only; it cannot replace the primary comparator, active-return series, inference, or decision rule.

time_index_contract.decision_index: t is the last actual NYSE trading session selected for rebalance, immediately after its official close.
time_index_contract.shared_signal_information_set: Candidate and primary matched comparator both use the same available return observations r[t-k] for k=0..h-1 at decision close t, separately for every locked horizon h.
time_index_contract.future_return_prohibition: No candidate or comparator signal, weight, volatility, covariance, cost, or decision input may use r[t+1] or any return after t.
time_index_contract.weight_index: Both portfolios compute their target weights at decision index t from their respective signals and the identical inherited non-signal inputs available at t.
time_index_contract.execution_index: Both portfolios execute their t-index targets at the official close of the next actual NYSE trading session t+1; same-close execution at t is forbidden.
time_index_contract.shared_portfolio_return_window: For each decision t, candidate and primary comparator net returns use the identical effective interval beginning immediately after their shared execution close at t+1 and ending immediately before the next shared executable rebalance close; the paired active return subtracts those two identical-window net returns.
time_index_contract.holiday_rule: If the next calendar day is not a NYSE session, t+1 means the next actual NYSE trading session; never manufacture a session.

primary_utility_and_inference.observation_unit: paired same-decision-index daily net active return: candidate and primary matched-horizon comparator net returns over the identical shared_portfolio_return_window
primary_utility_and_inference.primary_utility: annualized Sharpe of the paired daily net active-return series
primary_utility_and_inference.inference_input_rule: PSR, DSR, and MinTRL use only the same paired active-return observations generated from the shared decision and return windows.

search_accounting_and_DSR.DSR_input_series: For every locked trial, use paired same-decision-index daily net active returns over the identical shared_portfolio_return_window; its candidate and comparator share the trial's exact r[t-k] window.
search_accounting_and_DSR.trial_horizon_rule: Primary is 32/64/126/252; every leave-one-out sensitivity uses identical candidate and comparator horizon lists and the same time_index_contract.
search_accounting_and_DSR.selection_rule: Only the primary can support L-2; no sensitivity, L-1 60-day reference, or time-index variation may be promoted.

validation_seal.unlock_rule: No L-2 validation access in B5.2. A later execution order requires separate owner approval and a new locked gate.

hard_stops: L-2 return, signal, portfolio, or backtest execution during B5.2
broker or provider request, credential use, paid action, paper trade, or real-money action
in-place change to any locked preregistration or validator after hashing
edge, E2, execution-quality, deployment, or real-money claim from B5.2
